ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Dec-11-2019 Off Calendar

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Nov-14-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT WYNDHAM VACATION OWNERSHIP's MOTION TO SET ASIDE ENTRY OF DEFAULT.

Defendant Derek Milholland's motion to set aside entry of the default entered against him on August 12, 2019 is granted. Mr. Milholland claims that he was never personally served with the summons and complaint, and that the papers were left with his wife. (Milholland Decl. 3-4.) The registered process server in Hawaii who signed the proof of personal service says that he personally served Mr. Milholland after confirming his identity by name. (West Decl. 4.) There is reason to doubt Mr. Milholland's (or his counsel's) veracity. His counsel represented to Plaintiff's counsel that it was "impossible" that Mr. Milholland could have been personally served because he "did not live at the address listed on the proof of service form" (Higginbotham Decl. 14 & Ex. C), but Mr. Milholland squarely contradicts that assertion in his own declaration. (See Milholland Decl. 3.) However, the Court cannot definitively determine one way or the other whether Mr. Milholland was, in fact, properly served. The motion must be granted due to California's strong policy of deciding cases on their merits, Plaintiff's counsel's failure to notify Mr. Milholland or his counsel in advance of his intention to take the default, and the lack of any legal prejudice to Plaintiff Christopher Lapping by the granting of the motion. (See Elston v. City of Turlock (1985) 38 Cal.3d 227, 233 ["Because the law strongly favors trial and disposition on the merits, any doubts in applying section 473 must be resolved in favor of the party seeking relief from default."].) Mr. Milholland must file his answer or other responsive pleading within 10 days of this order.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

DEFENDANT WYNDHAM VACATION OWNDERSHIP, INC'S MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE.

The application of John K. Bennett to appear as counsel pro hac vice for Defendant Wyndham Vacation Ownership, Inc. is granted. Plaintiff has stipulated to granting the application, which conforms to California Rule of Court 9.40.

Order is signed.

MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
Off Calendar Mar-25-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT WYNDHAM VACATION OWNERSHIP, INC. MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE.

The motion to admit John K. Bennett pro hac vice is denied without prejudice to its renewal. The motion is not supported by a verified application by Mr. Bennett establishing that the requirements of CRC 9.40(d) have been met.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: W. Trupek, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)
